title: Guatteria moralesii
taxonomy: Guatteria moralesii is classified within the plant family Annonaceae. This plant family is commonly known as the custard apple family, consisting of over 1,600 species. Guatteria moralesii is a member of the Annonaceae family, which is native to Central and South America. The Annonaceae family is further divided into 131 genera, with Guatteria being one of them. Guatteria moralesii's classification is specifically within the genus Guatteria, which consists of approximately 90 species.
uses: Guatteria moralesii is reported to have various medicinal uses, including the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. It is also used to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation. Traditionally, the plant has been used to combat fever and infections. Additionally, extracts from Guatteria moralesii have been studied for their potential antimicrobial properties. However, further research is needed to confirm the full range of its medicinal applications.
